req.title: Search patients (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall search patients by name, MRN, or DOB; only patients in their care shall be returned. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-063 mandates that a clinician can search patients by name, MRN, or DOB with results filtered to their care panel, access logged, and malformed queries rejected with HTTP 400. Test design covers: (1) EP positive – valid name search returns only in-care patients with access log entry; (2) NEG – non-clinician role (patient role token) is denied access (403); (3) NEG – missing mandatory query parameter returns 400; (4) BVA – DOB at exact boundary (patient DOB equals search date) returns correct single match; (5) DT – conflicting state: search for a patient discharged from care returns empty bundle, not forbidden. Each test is self-contained via fixtures providing auth tokens and a deterministic patient dataset.

tests.name: test_clinician_name_search_returns_only_care_panel_patients
preconditions: Patient P001 (name=John Doe) is in C001 care panel
Patient P002 (name=John Doe) is NOT in C001 care panel
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: GET /Patient?search with header Authorization: Bearer <clinician_token> and query param name=John Doe
Capture HTTP status and response JSON body
Capture access log entry via audit endpoint
expected: Returned entry contains only patient P001
Access log contains a SearchPatient event for clinician C001
trace: R-HLT-063: clinician can search by name
tests.executable:
def test_clinician_name_search_returns_only_care_panel_patients(clinician_token, base_url, audit_client):
    resp = <http_get>(
        f"{base_url}/Patient?search",
        params={"name": "John Doe"},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["resourceType"] == "Bundle"
    assert bundle["total"] == 1
    entry_ids = [e["resource"]["id"] for e in bundle.get("entry", [])]
    assert "P001" in entry_ids
    assert "P002" not in entry_ids
    log = audit_client.latest_event(clinician_id="C001", action="SearchPatient")
    assert log is not None

tests.name: test_non_clinician_role_is_denied_patient_search
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: GET /Patient?search with header Authorization: Bearer <patient_role_token> and query param name=Jane
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code == forbidden
trace: R-HLT-063: only clinician role may search
tests.executable:
def test_non_clinician_role_is_denied_patient_search(patient_role_token, base_url):
    resp = <http_get>(
        f"{base_url}/Patient?search",
        params={"name": "Jane"},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_role_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    codes = [i["code"] for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert "forbidden" in codes

tests.name: test_search_with_no_query_param_returns_400
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: GET /Patient?search with valid clinician token but NO query parameters (name, mrn, dob all absent)
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code == required
trace: R-HLT-063: missing mandatory elements error pathway
tests.executable:
def test_search_with_no_query_param_returns_400(clinician_token, base_url):
    resp = <http_get>(
        f"{base_url}/Patient?search",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    codes = [i["code"] for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert "required" in codes

tests.name: test_dob_exact_boundary_match_returns_single_patient
preconditions: Patient P010 in C001 care panel with DOB exactly 2000-01-01
No other patient in C001 care panel shares DOB 2000-01-01
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param dob=2000-01-01 and valid clinician token
expected: Returned entry contains patient P010 with birthDate == 2000-01-01
trace: R-HLT-063: clinician can search by DOB
BVA: DOB equals boundary value of search date
tests.executable:
def test_dob_exact_boundary_match_returns_single_patient(clinician_token, base_url):
    resp = <http_get>(
        f"{base_url}/Patient?search",
        params={"dob": "2000-01-01"},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["total"] == 1
    patient = bundle["entry"][0]["resource"]
    assert patient["id"] == "P010"
    assert patient["birthDate"] == "2000-01-01"

tests.name: test_search_for_discharged_patient_returns_empty_bundle
preconditions: Patient P020 was previously in C001 care panel but has been discharged (state=discharged)
FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param mrn=MRN020 and valid clinician token
expected: No entry for patient P020 in the bundle
trace: R-HLT-063: only patients in their care shall be returned
R-HLT-063: conflicting state transition error pathway
STC: care-panel state transitions from active to discharged
tests.executable:
def test_search_for_discharged_patient_returns_empty_bundle(clinician_token, base_url):
    resp = <http_get>(
        f"{base_url}/Patient?search",
        params={"mrn": "MRN020"},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle["total"] == 0
    entry_ids = [e["resource"]["id"] for e in bundle.get("entry", [])]
    assert "P020" not in entry_ids

tests.name: test_malformed_dob_format_returns_400
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param dob=99-99-9999 (invalid date format) and valid clinician token
expected: Response OperationOutcome issue.code == invalid or value
No patient data leaked in response body
trace: DT: decision rule – valid role + invalid param value -> 400
tests.executable:
def test_malformed_dob_format_returns_400(clinician_token, base_url):
    resp = <http_get>(
        f"{base_url}/Patient?search",
        params={"dob": "99-99-9999"},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    codes = [i["code"] for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert any(c in codes for c in ("invalid", "value"))
    assert "entry" not in body
